executive_summary: LATAM mental health apps market valued at $452M (2024) growing at 14.6% CAGR to $1.06B by 2030; conservative SOM of $10.6M-$21.2M for AI-powered adolescent triage with 3 identifiable early adopter segments — score 68/100 (moderate opportunity).
score_reasoning:
Score: 68/100
- Data Availability & Source Quality: 19/25 (6+ market size estimates found from Grand View Research, MarketsandMarkets, Straits Research, IMARC Group, Statista, Precedence Research — all institutional, published 2024-2026, estimates converge within 2x for LATAM mental health apps: $452M-$500M range for 2024)
- Market Scale (SOM): 13/25 (SOM $10.6M-$21.2M — LATAM mental health apps TAM $452M in 2024; SAM filtered to adolescent segment ~15-20% = $68M-$90M; AI triage/teletherapy sub-segment ~50% of that = $34M-$45M; SOM at 2-3% of broader LATAM mental health apps TAM = $10.6M-$13.6M, or 5% of niche SAM = ~$21M; using conservative lower bound $10.6M places solidly in $10M-$100M tier)
- Growth Trajectory: 18/25 (median CAGR ~15-16% from multiple sources: Grand View Research 14.6% for LATAM mental health apps, MarketsandMarkets 18.0% global mental health apps, Straits Research 18.1% telepsychiatry; median of relevant figures ~16%; solidly in 10-24% tier, near top)
- Early Adopter Identifiability: 18/25 (3 segments meeting all 3 criteria with channels totaling 4.5M+ combined members — solidly in tier 13-18 near top)
Total: 19 + 13 + 18 + 18 = 68

evidence.quote: Latin America mental health apps market size was valued at USD 452.4 million in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 1,060.4 million by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 14.6% (2025-2030). Latin America represented approximately 6.0% of the global mental health apps market in 2024.
The Mental Health Apps Market is projected to grow from USD 9.94 billion in 2025 to USD 22.73 billion by 2030, at a CAGR of 18.0% (MarketsandMarkets, Feb 2026).

Global telepsychiatry market was USD 22.46 billion in 2024, projected to USD 100.38 billion by 2033 at CAGR 18.1%. Pediatric and adolescent segment identified as distinct market segment; teenager segment shows highest growth of 13.05%.
